title: Equivalence checking issue
body:
I am learning to use Yosys equivalence checking to compare verilog netlists. I've created a minimal design that fails equivalence checking when compared against itself. In this zip folder [not_equivalent.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/6858598/not_equivalent.zip) I have a .ys script that compares not_equivalent.v with itself. This yields the following output:
```
16. Executing EQUIV_STATUS pass.
Found 1 $equiv cells in equiv:
  Of those cells 0 are proven and 1 are unproven.
  Unproven $equiv $auto$equiv_make.cc:251:find_same_wires$6061: \lut6_inst.s5_2[3]_gold \lut6_inst.s5_2[3]_gate
Found a total of 1 unproven $equiv cells.
ERROR: Found 1 unproven $equiv cells in 'equiv_status -assert'.
```
The original verilog design (original_design.v) is also included in the zip folder. 

Also, here is a zip folder [equivalent.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/6858619/equivalent.zip) that is a similar design that passes as equivalent.

Is there a Yosys pass that I'm missing in my .ys file?
code_blocks:
16. Executing EQUIV_STATUS pass.
Found 1 $equiv cells in equiv:
  Of those cells 0 are proven and 1 are unproven.
  Unproven $equiv $auto$equiv_make.cc:251:find_same_wires$6061: \lut6_inst.s5_2[3]_gold \lut6_inst.s5_2[3]_gate
Found a total of 1 unproven $equiv cells.
ERROR: Found 1 unproven $equiv cells in 'equiv_status -assert'.
